description: Allow users to star or bookmark individual messages for personal reference, independent of channel-level pinning

actors.name: Authenticated User
actors.description: Any member who wants to bookmark messages for their own reference

actors.name: Messaging System
actors.description: Stores per-user star state and notifies clients of changes

general: Starring must be enabled at the system/workspace level; if disabled, no star or unstar actions are permitted
The acting user must be authenticated and have an active session
The user must be subscribed to the channel that contains the target message
The user must have active read access to the channel
Star state is stored per-user — starring a message does not affect other users' views
The same message can be starred by multiple users independently
If the starred message is the channel's last message, the channel's last-message metadata is updated to reflect the new star state
All star and unstar actions trigger extensibility hooks for downstream integrations
Starring does not modify the message content or its visibility to other users
Users can retrieve all their starred messages as a personal bookmark list

given: system-level message starring setting is disabled
starring_disabled.result: Action rejected; starring is not available in this workspace

user_not_authenticated.result: Action rejected; user must be logged in

given: user is not subscribed to the channel containing the message
not_subscribed.result: Action silently rejected; no star change recorded (not a member)

given: no message matching message_id exists in channel_id
message_not_in_channel.result: Action silently rejected; message does not exist in the specified channel

given: user cannot access the target channel
channel_not_accessible.result: Action rejected; user does not have access to this channel

given: user is authenticated and subscribed to the channel
user has access to the channel
star_added.result: Star added; the message appears in the user's personal starred list

given: user is authenticated and subscribed to the channel
user has access to the channel
star_removed.result: Star removed; the message is no longer in the user's starred list

errors.message: Message starring is not available in this workspace
You must be logged in to star messages
You do not have access to this channel

events.name: message.starred
events.description: Fired when a user adds a personal star to a message

events.name: message.unstarred
events.description: Fired when a user removes their personal star from a message

related.reason: Pinning is the channel-level equivalent; starring is per-user and private
Starred messages span channels and direct message threads

goals.description: Allow each user to maintain a private starred message list without affecting other users' views

success_metrics.measurement: Star operations that affect only the acting user / total star operations

human_checkpoints: before disabling starring workspace-wide for all users

invariants: starring a message does not modify the message content or its visibility to other users
star state is per-user and isolated from all other users
user must be subscribed to the channel to star messages within it

acceptance_tests.when: user B views the same message
acceptance_tests.expect: user B does not see user A's star

acceptance_tests.given: user is not subscribed to the channel containing the message

capabilities.description: Track and retrieve a per-user list of starred messages across channels

tradeoffs.reason: starred messages are personal bookmarks and must never be visible to other users
